issue: §10 says the worked example is 'five mode packets over the frozen deep-loop-runtime backend', but mode-registry.json's backendKind discriminator shows only 4 packets use runtime-loop-type (deep-loop-runtime); the deep-improvement packet runs over improvement-host (3 modes) and external-adapter (1 mode), NOT deep-loop-runtime (mode-registry.json:8,84,103,118,133). The hub template repeats the single-backend assumption ('All modes consume [backend]', parent_skill_hub_template.md:126-127).
fix: Qualify to 'five mode packets, four over the frozen deep-loop-runtime backend and the deep-improvement packet over improvement-host/external-adapter', consistent with the three backendKinds the registry defines.

issue: §10 anatomy (:1033-1035) and naming standard 'folder == packetSkillName == deep-<mode>' (:1042) imply one packet per mode, but the cited worked example has 8 workflowModes across 5 packets: deep-improvement hosts 4 modes (agent-improvement/model-benchmark/skill-benchmark/ai-system-improvement), none named deep-improvement, so 'deep-<mode>' cannot resolve. The many-modes-to-one-packet case is never surfaced (only ai-council's grandfather exception is).
fix: Add a sentence noting a packet may host multiple modes (deep-improvement hosts 4) and clarify the naming standard applies to single-mode packets; the multi-mode packet keeps one packetSkillName.
